title: Melissa Barrera's 2024 Horror Movie Becomes Streaming Success After $42M Box Office Disappointment
text: The 2024 horror film Abigail has found success on streaming platforms after disappointing theatrical returns. Directed by Scream franchise veterans Tyler Gillett and Matt Bettinelli-Olpin, the vampire movie stars Melissa Barrera as Joey, alongside Dan Stevens, Kathryn Newton, and other notable actors. The film currently ranks as the sixth most popular movie on streaming in the U.S. for the week of July 18–24, according to Reelgood data. It's available on Peacock Premium and has outperformed titles like Love Lies Bleeding and The Beekeeper. Critics embraced the film, which boasts an 82% on Rotten Tomatoes with an equally strong 85% audience score. Reviewers praised its humorous take on vampire tropes and the charismatic performances, particularly the blood-soaked finale. However, financial results told a different story. Despite a $28 million production budget, Abigail grossed only $42.4 million worldwide—far below the estimated $70 million break-even threshold when factoring in marketing and distribution costs. The primary issue was its relatively high budget for the horror genre. Recent vampire films like Renfield and Last Voyage of the Demeter also underperformed, suggesting declining theatrical interest in vampire movies. Interestingly, streaming performance may help offset theatrical losses through video-on-demand revenue.
